flashcards.front: What is the primary advantage of Tableau in big data visualization?
flashcards.back: Tableau offers drag-and-drop interface and seamless integration with Hadoop for handling petabyte-scale datasets.

flashcards.front: Why is D3.js preferred for custom visualizations?
flashcards.back: D3.js provides low-level control over SVG elements and dynamic data binding for bespoke visualizations.

flashcards.front: What makes Apache Superset suitable for distributed environments?
flashcards.back: Apache Superset integrates with Druid and supports cluster-based deployments for horizontal scalability.

flashcards.front: How does Grafana handle real-time data streams?
flashcards.back: Grafana connects to time-series databases like Prometheus and supports live dashboard refreshes.
QlikView's in-memory engine maintains data relationships for instant cross-filtering across visualizations.

flashcards.front: Why is data preprocessing crucial for visualization tools?
flashcards.back: Preprocessing reduces dimensionality and handles missing values to improve rendering performance.
Kepler.gl specializes in GPU-accelerated geospatial visualization of massive datasets using WebGL.

flashcards.front: How does Plotly enable collaborative analytics?
flashcards.back: Plotly provides shared dashboards with role-based access control and versioned visualizations.

flashcards.front: What is the main challenge in streaming data visualization?
flashcards.back: Maintaining low-latency rendering while ensuring consistency in rapidly changing data streams.

flashcards.front: Why use Power BI's DAX language?
flashcards.back: DAX enables complex time-intelligent calculations and business metrics across multiple data models.

flashcards.front: What is distributed rendering in visualization?
flashcards.back: Technique that splits rendering tasks across cluster nodes using frameworks like Spark GLV.

flashcards.front: How does TensorFlow Data Visualization (TFDV) help ML workflows?
flashcards.back: TFDV automatically detects data skews and anomalies in training datasets through visual profiling.
